tweets.text:
Announcing the winners of the https://t.co/xEPCcPiZHO hackathon.

A month ago, I put out a call for readers to build something cool with my podcast transcripts + newsletter content. The winner would get a free 1 year subscription to my newsletter.

With over 80+ entries, many that absolutely blew my mind, it was way too hard to pick just one winner. So I picked ten. Congrats to the winners!

In no particular order:
🔸 Lenny's Greatest Hits: https://t.co/TbxC8wo2Op
🔸 Lenny's Comics: https://t.co/DTBsASg3E2
🔸 Lenny's Roundtable: https://t.co/eDkBIWH3NL
🔸 Lenny's Books for Kids: https://t.co/CBAY2GDYtM
🔸 Lenny Unwrapped: https://t.co/e8VQ4ykHb0
🔸 The Re-Entry Brief: https://t.co/NoO5CbHZU1
🔸 Steelman advice skills: https://t.co/hcikHx9Na3
🔸 Lenny's Crystal Ball: https://t.co/DxNpRkfOpD
🔸 This Gets Me: https://t.co/eaaL8GzLLI
🔸 Lenny's Dots of Wisdom: https://t.co/I8gmw2fSAK

See comments on this post for all of the entries: https://t.co/yJDmuCQfOx

If you missed the contest, it's not too late to go play with the data and build something fun: https://t.co/xEPCcPiZHO.

And big thank you to @hbshih for inspiring this contest with his mind-blowing app https://t.co/Dyh61QvMGo.
tweets.author: lennysan
tweets.name: Lenny Rachitsky

tweets.text:
This may be the only high-level overview of OpenClaw on the internet.

Every time you search “OpenClaw” on YouTube, you get the same thing: deep technical setup guides, installation walkthroughs, and in the weeds tutorials.

As someone who loves podcasts but is short on time, I’ve struggled to find an OpenClaw video/podcast that I could listen to and didn’t have to visually follow along. I just wanted something to cover the "why" and the "what" of OpenClaw.

Then @lennysan released this episode with @clairevo 

It is the perfect high-level overview. They stay out of the weeds and discuss: What OpenClaw is. Why it’s so powerful. How Claire is actually using 8+ agents in her daily life.

If you're curious about OpenClaw, but aren't looking for a technical configuration guide, then I highly recommend giving this a listen:

https://t.co/m0QCi9bd7o
tweets.author: lennysan
tweets.name: Lenny Rachitsky

tweets.text:
I rarely do interviews or talk about my personal life, but I made an exception for the team at @firstround. 

Their writer spent hours at my house. We talked about why I started Lenny's Newsletter, what motivates me to keep building it, and a lot of things I don't usually share publicly. 

It's an intimate look at what my life is actually like outside of what most people see on the podcast.

Check it out: https://t.co/eNvWu269H0
tweets.author: lennysan
tweets.name: Lenny Rachitsky

tweets.text:
Had such a good time at the Parents in Product meet up with @TheRialMichelle  and @lennysan last night. (Charts for Babies is so cute buy at your local bookstore!!)

Met so many interesting founders, PMs, literally at the intersection of product and parenting — a teach your kid to read app, a vibe coding gaming platform for kids, a PM from @literati… and other PMs who are just also parents. 

Unexpected bonus: got some amazing feedback and advice on my own parenting product (ok ok it’s an Etsy shop but still I can pretend I’m a PM too)

tweets.author: lennysan
tweets.name: Lenny Rachitsky

tweets.text:
The word "agent" is the most overloaded term in AI right now.

Your backlog probably has 5-10 agent ideas. But one agent idea is a 6-week automation you can build with n8n. Another is a 6-month bet requiring a dedicated ML team. Putting them on the same spreadsheet and hoping impact-vs.-effort will sort it out doesn't work.

Hamza Farooq and Jaya Rajwani—who teach two of the most highly rated and well-respected courses on building AI agents—spent 50+ hours putting together a guide that'll help you make sense of the different categories of "agents," how to prioritize across them, and how to avoid common pitfalls—with recommended tools and many real-world examples.

Read this post the next time your team is going in circles on your agent strategy: 
https://t.co/2DDVIZgB5g
tweets.author: lennysan
tweets.name: Lenny Rachitsky

tweets.text:
Sure, everyone loves Claude Code but if you ask non-coders: they're all obsessed with Cowork.

@JJEnglert  at @tenex_labs is one of them, and he's giving us the power user's guide to getting started with @claudeai Cowork.

In this ep, JJ walks me through:
- How to set up Claude Cowork projects (hint: it's just a folder on your computer)
- His "brain" file: a doc that tells Claude who he is, how he works, and who he works with, so he never has to re-explain himself
- 3 AI agents with different personas that review your work before you share it with real humans

My favorite take from this ep: Cowork teaches you the mental models of Claude Code without ever opening a terminal.

If you've been stuck in chat mode wondering how to actually get AI to do work for you, this is your episode.

ty to our amazing sponsors!
🔀 @tines_hq  - Start building intelligent workflows today
🖱️@cursor_ai - The best way to code with AI 

Full episode: https://t.co/W5vPozVwLc

tweets.author: joulee
tweets.name: Julie Zhuo

tweets.text: Excellence over perfection.
tweets.author: joulee
tweets.name: Julie Zhuo

tweets.text:
Been enjoying an advance copy of @bgurley’s book “Runnin’ Down a Dream” which is out today!

So much of it resonates with my own experience (being told “hey you should be a doctor / lawyer / engineer” but in all my free time I’m drawing / writing / obsessively redesigning websites… all of which ultimately wove their way into my path in a beautiful way.) 

I’m also reading it from the parenting lens, and find it particularly timely for this next AI generation on the big questions of how to discover a career that brings joy and growth and success. 

Thank you Bill for this gem!
tweets.author: joulee
tweets.name: Julie Zhuo
